atDirectives.name: @theme
atDirectives.description: Use the @theme directive to define your project's custom design tokens (colors, fonts, spacing, etc.). Tailwind generates utilities and CSS variables from these values.

atDirectives.name: @tailwind
atDirectives.description: Use the @tailwind directive to insert Tailwind's base, components, utilities and variants styles into your CSS.

atDirectives.name: @apply
atDirectives.description: Use the @apply directive to inline any existing utility classes into your own custom CSS.

atDirectives.name: @layer
atDirectives.description: Use the @layer directive to tell Tailwind which 'bucket' a set of custom styles belong to (base, components, or utilities).

atDirectives.name: @variant
atDirectives.description: Use the @variant directive to apply a Tailwind variant to styles in your own CSS.

atDirectives.name: @custom-variant
atDirectives.description: Use the @custom-variant directive to add a custom variant in your project.

atDirectives.name: @utility
atDirectives.description: Use the @utility directive to add custom utilities to your project that work with Tailwind's variants like hover, focus and responsive breakpoints.

atDirectives.name: @source
atDirectives.description: Use the @source directive to explicitly register source files that Tailwind should scan for class names.

atDirectives.name: @config
atDirectives.description: Use the @config directive to load a legacy JavaScript-based configuration file.

atDirectives.name: @plugin
atDirectives.description: Use the @plugin directive to load a legacy JavaScript-based plugin.

atDirectives.name: @reference
atDirectives.description: Use the @reference directive to import your main CSS file for reference without duplicating its output (useful in component <style> blocks).
